skill.name: marketing-ideas
skill.description: Provide proven marketing strategies and growth ideas for SaaS and software products, prioritized using a marketing feasibility scoring system.
skill.summary: Prioritizes SaaS marketing ideas with feasibility scoring and execution guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged five external command patterns, one weak cryptography pattern, and one reconnaissance pattern. Manual review found these are false positives in Markdown instructions, score formulas, and marketing guidance, with no executable code or prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Formulas Misidentified as Commands
low_findings.description: The static external command findings are fenced Markdown examples for the Marketing Feasibility Score formula and score range. No shell, Ruby, or executable command behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are Markdown code fences, a score range, and an arithmetic example. They do not invoke a shell or any external process.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The high severity weak cryptography alert at the frontmatter description is not supported by file content. No cryptographic function, hashing algorithm, encryption call, or security-sensitive computation appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is a natural language skill description about marketing strategies. I found no evidence of cryptographic code or algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert points to stage-based marketing guidance that mentions paid acquisition, partnerships, and product-led growth loops. No host, network, user, or system discovery instruction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is plain marketing strategy text, not an instruction to inspect system state. The surrounding section also stays within marketing planning guidance.

content.value_statement: SaaS teams often have more marketing ideas than time, budget, or execution capacity. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code select, score, and operationalize the most feasible growth ideas.
actual_capabilities: Asks for product, customer, stage, budget, team, and goal context before recommending tactics.
Shortlists six to ten relevant SaaS marketing ideas from a curated strategy library.
Scores each recommendation with impact, effort, cost, speed, and fit dimensions.
Limits final recommendations to the top three to five ideas with positive feasibility scores.
Provides first steps, success metrics, expected outcomes, resource needs, and execution risks.
limitations: It does not run campaigns, buy ads, publish content, or integrate with marketing tools.
It depends on user-provided business context and does not verify market data independently.
It omits the full 140 idea library in the visible skill file and references it as assumed context.
It provides strategic guidance, not guaranteed revenue outcomes or legal compliance advice.

use_cases.title: Plan early traction tests
use_cases.description: Choose low-cost marketing experiments that can show signal quickly before a SaaS launch.

use_cases.title: Prioritize growth backlog items
use_cases.description: Compare SEO, paid acquisition, partnership, and product-led ideas using a consistent score.

use_cases.title: Align team execution
use_cases.description: Convert selected marketing ideas into first steps, metrics, resources, and risk notes.

prompt_templates.title: Get starter ideas
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an early-stage SaaS for [customer type]. Suggest marketing ideas and ask any missing questions before scoring them.

prompt_templates.title: Score a shortlist
prompt_templates.prompt: Score these marketing ideas for my SaaS using the Marketing Feasibility Score: [ideas]. My budget is [budget], team is [team], and goal is [goal].

prompt_templates.title: Build a 30-day test plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend the top three marketing ideas for the next 30 days. Include MFS, first steps, success metrics, resources, and execution risks.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize channel strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as a SaaS growth operator. Given our ICP, stage, funnel data, budget, and team constraints, rank feasible channels and explain what to defer.

output_examples.input: We sell a B2B onboarding SaaS to customer success teams and need low-budget lead generation ideas.
output_examples.output: A concise ranked list with feasibility scores, likely led by founder-led LinkedIn outreach, comparison content, and customer success community participation.

output_examples.input: Compare programmatic SEO, paid search, webinars, and partner co-marketing for a growth-stage SaaS.
output_examples.output: A scored comparison that explains fit, cost, speed to signal, expected outcomes, resource needs, and which idea should be tested first.

output_examples.input: Create a launch marketing plan for a pre-launch developer tool with one founder and no ad budget.
output_examples.output: A prioritized plan focused on fast learning channels such as waitlist content, founder distribution, community posts, and direct user interviews.

best_practices: Provide product stage, ideal customer, budget, team size, and primary growth goal.
Ask for a ranked shortlist instead of a long list of unrelated tactics.
Use the feasibility score as a decision tool, then validate with real campaign data.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for generic ideas without business context or constraints.
Do not treat high-impact ideas as automatic priorities when effort and cost are too high.
Do not request more than five final recommendations when the goal is execution focus.

faq.question: What type of products is this skill best for?
faq.answer: It is designed for SaaS and software products, especially teams choosing growth experiments under constraints.

faq.question: Does it generate full marketing campaigns?
faq.answer: It provides strategy, prioritization, first steps, metrics, and risks. It does not execute campaigns.

faq.question: What is the Marketing Feasibility Score?
faq.answer: It is a simple score that weighs impact, fit, and speed against effort and cost.

faq.question: Can it help with paid acquisition decisions?
faq.answer: Yes. It can score paid acquisition against budget, speed, fit, and expected return constraints.

faq.question: Does it require analytics data?
faq.answer: No, but analytics data improves recommendations and helps validate the selected ideas.

faq.question: Can Claude Code or Codex use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
